label: US-relevant macro-financial stress outside NBER recessions, evaluated by dimension
geography: United States with documented global spillovers
scopePolicy: Episodes cannot overlap NBER recession months. Dimensions are evaluated before composite regime alignment. V1 episodes are development evidence; new v2 episodes form a protected diagnostic partition and cannot authorize promotion.

sources.name: BIS Quarterly Review December 2011
sources.role: documents global spillovers from euro-area sovereign stress

sources.name: BIS Quarterly Review September 2013 - Markets precipitate tightening
sources.role: documents the May-June 2013 cross-asset shock and market-led tightening

sources.name: BIS Quarterly Review September 2015
sources.role: documents the August 2015 China and emerging-market shock

sources.name: Federal Reserve 2018 Annual Report
sources.role: documents fourth-quarter risk repricing and tighter financial conditions

sources.name: Federal Reserve - What Happened in Money Markets in September 2019?
sources.role: documents acute repo and federal-funds market pressure in September 2019 and stabilization measures

sources.name: FRED CPIAUCSL
sources.role: supports the CPI year-over-year episode boundary

sources.name: Federal Reserve Monetary Policy Report June 2022
sources.role: documents broad inflation and tightening financial conditions

sources.name: Federal Reserve Financial Stability Report May 2023
sources.role: documents regional-bank stress and subsequent stabilization

taxonomy.label: Financial-system or broad market stress
taxonomy.definition: Documented disruption, flight to quality, material risk repricing or funding-market stress.

taxonomy.label: Growth scare
taxonomy.definition: Documented material concern about weakening growth outside an NBER recession month.

taxonomy.label: Inflation shock
taxonomy.definition: Headline CPI year-over-year at or above 4 percent with broad institutional evidence.

taxonomy.label: Rapid monetary tightening
taxonomy.definition: Federal Reserve hiking cycle or documented market-led tightening shock.

episodes.name: Euro-area sovereign stress spillover
episodes.boundaryRationale: BIS dates the dominant market-stress window from September through early December; monthly coverage includes September-December.

episodes.name: Taper-announcement market shock
episodes.boundaryRationale: BIS documents the May announcement shock, the cross-asset selloff and pressure through June, followed by reassurance and easing in early July.

episodes.name: China and emerging-market growth stress
episodes.boundaryRationale: The August 2015 shock was followed by renewed turbulence in January-February 2016.

episodes.name: Fourth-quarter 2018 risk repricing
episodes.boundaryRationale: The Federal Reserve documents marked deterioration in risk appetite and risky-asset prices from October through December.

episodes.name: US repo-market stress
episodes.boundaryRationale: Rates spiked in mid-September; Federal Reserve operations stabilized overnight markets while measures continued into October.

episodes.name: Post-pandemic inflation shock
episodes.boundaryRationale: Headline CPI year-over-year remained at or above 4 percent from April 2021 through May 2023.

episodes.name: Federal Reserve rapid tightening cycle
episodes.boundaryRationale: The first target-range increase occurred in March 2022 and the final increase in July 2023.

episodes.name: US regional banking stress
episodes.boundaryRationale: Acute bank stress began in March; the May report documents stabilization with residual stress.

limitations: The chronology is curated ex post and is never a real-time model input.
Thresholds describe normalized dimensions and are not calibrated probabilities.
The protected-v2 partition prevents silent reuse of only v1 episodes but remains part of an already observed historical benchmark.
Absence of a label does not establish a true negative.
No result from this chronology can authorize operational promotion without fresh shadow-live evidence.
